_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Surgical Correction of Category III Congenital Heart Disease - Arch interruption Repair without VSD closure
clinical_indications: Interrupted aortic arch (IAA) is an anomaly that can be considered the most severe form of aortic coarctation.
IAA involves an anatomical and luminal disruption between the ascending and descending aorta.
IAA is a ductus dependent lesion, requiring blood flow via the ductus arteriosus to distal parts.

Associated lesions may include: posterior malalignment of the conal septum, left ventricular outflow tract obstruction, transposition of the great arteries, truncus arteriosus, aortopulmonary window, single ventricle, aortic valve atresia, right-sided ductus, and double-outlet right ventricle.

Clinical features indicating need for surgery (typically appearing after ductus arteriosus closes): tachypnea, feeding difficulties, respiratory distress, cyanosis, anuria, leading to shock and death.
Physical exam reveals absent pulses with a difference in blood pressure between the right arm and lower extremities.
Oxygen discrepancy between the left and right side of the body may also be present.

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure
Was the echo/Doppler report suggestive of interrupted aortic arch?

cpd_claim.q: Are the indoor case papers submitted
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show repair of the defect?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Is the barcode of implant used submitted?

common_queries: Echo/Doppler report not suggestive of interrupted aortic arch or diagnosis unclear.
Clinical notes missing or lacking detailed history, signs, symptoms, or indication for procedure.
Post-procedure ECHO stills do not clearly show repair of the defect.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or does not mention date of follow-up.
Barcode of implant not submitted, if an implant was used in the procedure.

surgical_options: Repair using prosthetic vascular graft or patch.
